description: OWASP Agentic AI Top 10 mapped to both MITRE ATT&CK tactics/techniques and AI alignment concepts

mappings.owasp_description: An attacker manipulates the AI agent through crafted inputs, causing unintended actions, bypassing safety mechanisms, or hijacking the agent's decision-making process.
mappings.description: The most direct mapping across all four frameworks. Prompt injection is simultaneously Initial Access (ATT&CK), an ATLAS-cataloged technique, the top OWASP agentic risk, and a fundamental alignment failure (the model cannot reliably distinguish instructions from data).

mappings.owasp_description: The AI agent inadvertently exposes sensitive information through its responses, logs, or interactions with external services.
mappings.description: Data leakage maps to ATT&CK's Exfiltration tactic with AI-specific channels (model outputs, tool calls, generated artifacts). In alignment terms, this includes both adversarial extraction and emergent leakage from memorized training data.

mappings.owasp_description: The AI agent has more permissions, tools, or autonomy than necessary for its intended function, creating unnecessary risk.
mappings.description: Excessive agency is the AI-specific violation of the security principle of least privilege. It maps to Privilege Escalation because the agent effectively operates at a higher privilege level than its task requires, expanding the blast radius of any compromise.

mappings.owasp_description: An attacker gains elevated privileges within the AI agent system through credential theft, authorization bypass, or exploitation of trust relationships.
mappings.description: Privilege compromise in agentic systems maps to both Credential Access (stealing API keys, tokens) and Privilege Escalation (exploiting trust relationships between agents). In alignment terms, this includes corrupting the safety mechanisms that constrain agent behavior.

mappings.owasp_description: The AI agent's outputs are not properly validated, sanitized, or constrained before being used in downstream systems.
mappings.description: Improper output handling creates the execution pathway for agent compromises. In ATT&CK terms, the lack of output validation is the vulnerability; in alignment terms, it is the absence of the verification layer between model outputs and real-world actions.

mappings.owasp_description: The AI agent retrieves and processes external data without adequate validation, enabling data poisoning and indirect prompt injection.
mappings.description: Unsafe retrieval is the RAG-specific attack surface. In ATT&CK terms, the knowledge base is the supply chain, and poisoning it is a supply chain compromise. In alignment terms, the retrieval system violates the principle that untrusted data should never be treated as trusted instructions.

mappings.owasp_description: The AI agent reveals its system prompt, internal instructions, or operational parameters to unauthorized parties.
mappings.description: System prompt leakage maps to both Credential Access (the system prompt is effectively a credential/configuration) and Discovery (revealing the system's constraints enables further attacks). In alignment terms, it reveals the safety constraints, enabling targeted evasion.

mappings.owasp_description: An attacker causes the AI agent to consume excessive resources through crafted inputs, recursive operations, or amplification attacks.
mappings.description: DoS/resource drain in agentic systems can be both adversarial (attacker-triggered) and emergent (misalignment-caused). The alignment lens adds the insight that resource exhaustion can arise from specification errors in the agent's task decomposition, not just from external attacks.

mappings.owasp_description: Users or systems over-trust AI agent outputs without verification, leading to propagation of errors, hallucinations, or manipulated information.
mappings.description: Overreliance is the human-side failure that enables AI misalignment to cause real-world harm. In ATT&CK terms, it is the social engineering of the human operator. In alignment terms, it is the failure of scalable oversight -- the human cannot effectively supervise the AI's outputs.

mappings.owasp_description: The AI agent exhibits behaviors that diverge from its intended objectives due to training issues, reward hacking, or emergent goals.
mappings.description: OWASP's catch-all for alignment failures. This maps to ATT&CK's Defense Evasion (deceptive alignment) and Impact (reward hacking). It is the most alignment-native category in the OWASP Agentic Top 10, encompassing the core concerns of the alignment research community.
